title: blumea decurrens
intro: Blumea decurrens, also known as the annual or biennial fleabane, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Asia and Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The plant is known to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Blumea decurrens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
